untranslatable_reason: Signal requires comparison of Implied Volatility (IV) against a trader's forecast volatility, and IV rank/percentile thresholds. These are options-market specific indicators not in the allowed list (india_vix is a volatility index proxy but not true implied volatility from option chains). The entry/exit thresholds (iv_to_forecast_multiple, min_iv_percentile, profit_target_pct_of_premium) are parameterized and cannot be translated without specific values. Historical volatility (hvol_20) and india_vix are used as proxies but do not accurately represent option implied volatility or IV percentile rank.
translation_notes: Attempted proxy: used india_vix (equity volatility index) as a substitute for implied volatility and hvol_20 (20-day historical volatility) as a forecast volatility proxy. Entry conditions check if current vol/VIX is above historical vol AND above 75th percentile threshold. Exit when vol drops below forecast level. Signal is fundamentally an options volatility-selling strategy requiring IV data not available in price-action indicators. Manual parameter tuning required with actual IV data.
